---
title: "Abhishek Baraili"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    theme: cosmo
    page-layout: full
execute:
  echo: false
---

I am Abhishek Baraili, a final-year economics undergraduate. I write about my
interests here, mostly short projects on applied statistics and causal inference,
worked through in R, Python, Stata, and QGIS. The data is usually Nepali, but the
methods are the real interest. Some work, some don't. Below is a list of my writing.

[GitHub](https://github.com/abhishekbaraili) · [Email](mailto:youremail@example.com)

---

### Writing

- [How do I turn an earthquake into one number per municipality?](zonal_stats_nepal_v3.html) &nbsp; **24 June 2026**

    Before I could use the 2015 Gorkha earthquake's shaking intensity as an
    instrument, I had to solve a smaller, dumber problem: turning a grid of shaking
    values into one number per municipality. A short walkthrough of zonal statistics
    in Python, and the measurement mistake I nearly made.
